process_timeline.action: Read README_EN.md, Makefile, Dockerfile-atune-engine, go.mod to extract build/test/sample info
Start Ubuntu 24.04 Docker container for isolated verification
Install system dependencies (golang, python3, perf, sysstat, hwloc, lshw, sqlite3)
Execute 'make all' - build atune-adm, atuned, modules (plugin), and db
Attempt pip install of Python dependencies for samples/tests
process_timeline.result: failed - network too slow (9-30 kB/s for large packages: xgboost 131MB, scipy 35MB)

process_timeline.action: Test built binaries (atune-adm, atuned) and database
Attempt to run test suite (make check)
Attempt to run model generation and tuning samples
process_timeline.result: partially successful - CLI commands work; model generation skipped (Python deps not available)

warnings: sqlite3-binding.c: In function 'sqlite3SelectNew': warning: function may return address of local variable (vendored sqlite3 code, non-fatal)

artifacts.name: atuned.db
artifacts.description: SQLite database with 8 tables (class_apps, class_profile, collection, profile_log, rule_tuned, schedule, tuned, tuned_item)

ut.failure_reason: All 25 test scripts (test_atune-adm_*.sh, test_atuned_cnf_*.sh, test_atuned_start_stop.sh, test_translate_tuning_yamls.sh, test_use_make_*.sh) require systemd service management (systemctl start/status/restart atuned). Standard Docker containers do not run systemd as PID 1. Tests could be executed in a systemd-enabled container (e.g., using --privileged with /sbin/init) or on a bare metal / VM with openEuler.

results.name: atune-adm list
results.output: Cannot connect to atuned daemon (service not started)

results.name: generate_models.py
results.output: Python dependencies (pandas, scikit-learn, scikit-optimize, xgboost) not installed due to slow network (PyPI downloads at 9-30 kB/s)

results.name: tuning_examples (27 scenarios in ./examples/tuning/)
results.output: Tuning examples require atuned daemon running and full installation (make install)

documentation_gaps: Architecture requirements not explicitly stated in README - only OS (openEuler) is mentioned
Go minimum version (1.14) only found in go.mod, not mentioned in README build instructions
Python dependency versions not specified (only package names listed)
Test execution instructions not in README - only found in Makefile (make check target)
No Docker Compose or containerized test setup documented for running integration tests without bare metal
README build instructions assume openEuler with yum - no apt/dnf agnostic instructions for other distros
The Makefile 'db' target requires sqlite3 CLI, but this is not listed as a dependency in README
No contributing guide (CONTRIBUTING.md) for developer workflow
The atune-engine Dockerfile uses python:3.8-slim as base, but main README only documents openEuler installation

problems_encountered.problem: PyPI packages very slow to download (9-30 kB/s) in Docker container
problems_encountered.solution: Build was not blocked (Go build uses vendored deps). Python deps only needed for runtime/analysis features. Could use apt packages or pre-downloaded wheels for faster install.
problems_encountered.source: Observed during pip install - network limitation

problems_encountered.problem: Tests require systemd services (systemctl start atuned)
problems_encountered.solution: Tests cannot run in standard Docker. Need either: (a) systemd-enabled container (docker run --privileged with /sbin/init), (b) bare metal / VM with openEuler, or (c) refactored tests with mock service layer for CI.
problems_encountered.source: All 25 test scripts in tests/ directory call check_service_started() or systemctl directly

problems_encountered.problem: pip externall-managed-environment error on Ubuntu 24.04 Python 3.12
problems_encountered.solution: Used --break-system-packages flag to bypass PEP 668 restriction. This is a known issue with newer Ubuntu/Python versions when mixing apt and pip packages.
problems_encountered.source: Python 3.12 PEP 668 - observed during pip install

problems_encountered.solution: Non-fatal warning in vendored go-sqlite3 C code. Does not affect functionality. Upstream fix would require updating vendored github.com/mattn/go-sqlite3.
problems_encountered.source: Observed during go build of atuned module
